data.title: Nvidia supplier SK hynix reports record Q3 profit amid solid demand for AI servers
data.description:
SK hynix (OTCPK:HXSCF) reported posted record quarterly results, driven by strong demand for its high bandwidth memory (HBM) chips used in generative AI.

The South Korean company, which is a key supplier to Nvidia (NVDA), generated 17.57 trillion won in Q3 revenues, 7.03 trillion won in operating profit (with an operating margin of 40%), and net profit of 5.75 trillion won (with a net margin of 33%) - all reaching new records.

Quarter revenues marked an all-time high, exceeding the previous record of 16.42 trillion won in the second quarter of this year by more than 1 trillion won.

SK hynix (OTCPK:HXSCF) emphasized that the demand for AI memory continued to be strong, centered on data center customers. In particular, HBM sales showed solid growth, up more than 70% from the previous quarter and more than 330% from the same period last year.

As sales increased mainly on highly profitable premium products, the average selling price of both DRAM and NAND chips rose in the mid 10% range compared to the previous quarter, which made the company mark the highest operating profit.

“While the demand of memory for AI servers such as HBM and [high-density enterprise SSD] has grown noticeably this year, the company predicts that this trend will continue next year,” the firm said in a press release.

data.title: Bit Digital rated new Buy at B. Riley on growing AI business
data.description:
B. Riley started coverage on Bit Digital (NASDAQ:BTBT) stock with a Buy rating on the basis of the company's "major transformation" over the last year with the expansion of its AI business, according to a Wednesday note.

In a bid to diversify its revenue beyond bitcoin (BTC-USD) mining, Bit Digital (BTBT) rolled out its AI business nearly a year ago. It has "already achieved a $50M revenue run-rate [in the GPU segment], and we expect it to achieve a $100M run-rate within the next two quarters from its existing customer base," analyst Nick Giles wrote.

"While the digital assets segment serves as an important call option on higher BTC and ETH prices, we believe separating this business in the medium term could also accelerate this re-rating," he said, adding that Bit Digital's (BTBT) current valuation fails to fully reflect the company's shift toward more stable revenue.

B. Riley's Buy rating diverges from the SA Quant system rating of Hold and aligns with the average Wall Street analyst rating of Strong Buy.

(BTBT) shares gained 2.5% in Thursday afternoon trading.

data.title: Bread Financial credit card delinquencies rise, charge-offs fall in September
data.description:
Bread Financial's (NYSE:BFH) credit card delinquency rate rose as net charge-offs declined in September, according to the company's performance update released on Thursday.

Its delinquency rate increased to 6.4% from 6.2% in August and 6.3% a year ago. Its net charge-off rate of 7.4% dropped from 7.8% in the previous month and increased from 6.7% in September 2023.

Lending activity was flat with August at $17.9B in credit card and other loans as of Sept. 30, 2024, the same as a year ago.

data.title: Harbour Energy to put North Sea assets up for sale as tax hikes loom - report
data.description:
Harbour Energy's (OTCPK:PMOIF) plans to sell stakes in its U.K. North Sea oilfields is a "stark warning" to the U.K. government, politicians warned Thursday, amid tax hikes expected to be announced in the government's budget later this month.

Reuters reported Harbour (OTCPK:PMOIF) wants to divest from projects on the U.K. continental shelf and is preparing for a U.S. market listing, which could pave the way to move the company's headquarters from its current location in London.

A potential move was paused when Harbour (OTCPK:PMOIF) decided late last year to acquire Wintershall Dea's non-Russian portfolio for $11B, according to the report; the deal, which allowed Harbour to diversify outside the North Sea and more than doubled its production, was completed last month.

Harbour (OTCPK:PMOIF) also reportedly has launched a sale process of its stakes in the Armada, Everest, Lomond, Catcher and Tolmount fields in the North Sea.

data.title: U.S. Bancorp rating cut, Raymond James raised at Deutsche Bank
data.description:
Deutsche Bank downgraded U.S. Bancorp USB) to Hold from Buy as analyst Matt O'Connor assumes the stock will trade mostly in line with the large regional bank group.

Conversely, he upgraded Raymond James Financial (NYSE:RJF) to Buy from Hold as he revised the stock's expected discount to its peers to 5% vs. 10%-15% previously. The smaller discount reflects reduced risk, O'Connor added.

Raymond James (NYSE:RJF) stock jumped 6.6% in Thursday midday trading, and U.S. Bancorp (NYSE:USB) stock dipped 0.5%.

He puts the large regional bank group's relative multiple at 60% to the S&P 500, the lower end of the sector's typical range of 60%-75% during a non-recessionary period.

O'Connor reasoned that the lower end is warranted given higher-than-normal macro risks and higher-than-normal valuations for the broader market, which is skewed by a handful of large stocks.

U.S.Bancorp (NYSE:USB) price target stays at $51. Deutsche Bank increased Raymond James's (RJF) price target to $26 from $24.

The Hold rating on USB aligns with the SA Quant rating of Hold and contrasts with the average Wall Street rating of Buy.

His Buy rating on RJF contrasts with the SA Quant rating of Hold and aligns with the average Wall Street rating of Buy.
